synthesis.summary: CF Threat Watch flagged a brute-force-suspect pattern (401×37 then 40×2xx SUCCESS logins) from a single IP on ft-crm.com. Joachim acknowledged instantly; escalation to the security on-call (Max, Agustín) had paging misses (Max unavailable, Agustín's escalation expired) but both joined the channel manually within ~4 minutes anyway via Stephen Riolo, who took over as lead. Team is jointly investigating via Cloudflare security analytics; current read is a shared-network false positive, not an attack — CF itself classifies it as 'not an exploit'.
key_events: 16 Jul 12:10:44 UTC alert fires, Joachim acknowledges within seconds

12:11:36-12:14:59 UTC escalation to security on-call: Max paged but unavailable, Agustín's escalation expired, Stephen Riolo acknowledges and becomes lead; Max and Agustín both join the channel regardless

12:21-12:39 UTC joint investigation via Cloudflare dashboard; Agustín: 'doesn't look like an attack and CF is classifying it as not an exploit'; Stephen: 'don't think this is an attack... more some shared network'
Still actively investigating at check time (~29min old)
16 Jul 23:42 CEST bot nudge asking to mark resolved -- no human response yet (overnight, expected)
proposals: Verify via Cloudflare security analytics + bot session double-check - in progress
synthesis.why: Fast ack and correct B6 security escalation flow (SOC→SRE→security team), even though the paging itself misfired (Max unavailable, Agustín's escalation expired) — both engaged within ~4 minutes regardless. Too fresh and too well-handled to flag; current evidence points to a false positive.

synthesis.summary: Leonbets activity-manager and activity-manager-lifecycles consumer groups were spamming slow-processing alerts (SOC noise), traced by Matteo to a shared Aurora writer being saturated by full-brand config reloads (leonbets has 598k actions, ~7.9M rows read per pod per reload). Matteo deployed a temporary kubectl-level fix (scoped action loading + longer reload interval), cutting RDS CPU from 94-99% to 40-50% and lag from ~94k/77k to <2k on each group within ~90 minutes of investigation starting. Set to Monitoring at 12:16:59 CEST.
key_events: 16 Jul 10:47 UTC alert fires (pair of alerts, 2 related incidents auto-merged)
12:47-13:20 CEST Matteo root-causes: DB saturation from unscoped config reload, not a Kafka/consumer issue; two prior scale-up attempts (partitions, pod count) made it worse
13:20-14:09 CEST Matteo drafts and deploys PR #490 (scoped action loading) as a kubectl-level change, plus bumped lifecycles reload interval to 300s
14:16:59 CEST incident updated with full root-cause writeup and measured impact; status → Monitoring
Explicit note in the update: today's kubectl-level changes revert on the next OneClick deploy unless PR #490 is merged and the env vars persisted
No new human activity this cycle (bot nudge only) -- PR #490 merge / EV persistence still not confirmed done
proposals: Scoped action loading (PR #490) - applied via kubectl today, durable merge+EV persistence still pending
Lifecycles reload interval 120s→300s - applied, durable EV persistence still pending
open_breaches: A2: no incident.io follow-up yet tracking the durable fix (merge PR #490 + persist the two EV settings) — the applied fix reverts on the next deploy if this isn't done
synthesis.why: Excellent technical handling — real root cause, quantified impact, working mitigation within ~90 minutes — but the durable half of the fix (merging the PR, persisting config) isn't yet tracked as a follow-up, and today's kubectl changes are explicitly noted to revert on the next deploy.
synthesis.suggested_action: Create an incident.io follow-up for merging PR #490 and persisting the two EV settings before this incident closes, so the fix survives the next OneClick deploy.

INC-14331.name: Leonbets Activity Manager Groups Alerts Spamming

synthesis.summary: ibetcrm Activity Manager Fire service stopped consuming its queue. Joachim root-caused it well within ~1h30m (NULL values in analytics.user_consents.post_mail crashing the bool parser — 557 of 674,216 rows for brand 131), fixed the data, and the incident was resolved and closed at 13:39 CEST. It recurred within the hour: a new alert (31 messages) fired and auto-merged in at 14:24 CEST, reopening the incident to Investigating. Joachim is actively investigating the recurrence.

16 Jul 14:59 CEST the recurrence alert auto-resolved
No new activity this cycle (bot nudge only) -- patch_ibet.json alias fix still not confirmed shipped
proposals: NULL→0 data fix for brand 131 - applied, held for ~45min then the same alert class recurred
analytics-config v2.67.2 patch_ibet.json alias fix (NULL not '') — identified, not yet applied; code hardening already in dev PR #329

synthesis.why: The recurrence has now been thoroughly root-caused with a concrete fix and a hardening PR already in flight — good handling, but confirm the patch_ibet.json fix actually ships so it doesn't recur a third time.
synthesis.suggested_action: Ship the patch_ibet.json alias fix (NULL, not empty string) and merge PR #329's defensive guard; watch for a third recurrence before considering this closed.

synthesis.summary: CRM login unavailable across all brands due to a WorkOS (3rd-party auth) outage. Instant ack, root cause in ~1 minute, Patrik and Andrea both actively driving response throughout. Partial recovery noticed ~12:09 CEST with most brands recovering; comms sent to partners twice (12:11 and 12:19 CEST). One partner-facing residual issue (login codes delayed by the WorkOS backlog, 7-9min) surfaced at 13:49 CEST via a new partner (CasinoOK) report and was resolved by 14:34 CEST with clear guidance (wait ~10min, use only the latest code). Status moved to Monitoring at 12:26 CEST.

synthesis.summary: Stanleybet Romania (partner) reported users showing as 'unsubscribed' in activity reporting (e.g. CRsorin, valentinspetcu on ACT-23150) despite being subscribed and no unsubscribe events received. First raised via Intercom support 15 Jul 13:07 UTC, escalated to SRE 16 Jul 12:41 UTC after support couldn't resolve it. Giancarlo picked it up within 10 minutes and root-caused it within ~46 minutes as a reporting false-positive (a consent event not checking opt-in/opt-out direction, miscounting as unsubscribe) — no real unsubscribe/consent bug. Andrea joined the channel and flagged this may be the same pattern as the Betiro no-unsubs incident (INC-13738) and another informal unsub discussion elsewhere.

synthesis.summary: Wiz security scan flagged a critical Log4Shell (CVE-2021-45046) finding on the kafka-ui container image (staging cluster, sortenabet namespace) -- runtime-validated, CISA KEV-listed, 100% EPSS. Gerard investigated, found contradictory exposure signals (ASG shows no internet exposure, but some instances open to 0.0.0.0/0), and per the runbook's own guidance ('a false escalation costs minutes, a missed P1 costs far more') escalated to security on-call. Paging misfired (Andrea's escalation expired, Giancarlo paged but silent) but Patrik acknowledged and joined directly, reviewed it personally, then handed the lead back to Gerard and downgraded P1->P3 with 'this can wait until tomorrow.'
